Let V be a K –vector space and G a finite group. A linear representation of G is a group homomorphism \rho :G\to GL(V)=Aut(V) Here GL(V) is notation for a general linear group, and Aut(V) for an automorphism group. This means that a linear representation is a map \rho :G\to GL(V) which satisfies \rho(st)=\rho(s)\rho(t) for all s,t\in G

In other words, ρ has to be one of the following three maps:

three case formulas

Let G=Z/2Z × Z/2Z and let \rho:G→GL₂(C) be the group homomorphism defined by:

matrix formula

In other words, the following diagram commutes for all s∈G :

Such a map is also called G –linear , or an equivariant map.
